user_prompt: Our privacy policy is 2 years old. Generate a checklist of what to update for current GDPR + ePrivacy.
actual_output:
## Privacy Policy Update Checklist — GDPR + ePrivacy

*Compliance guidance only, not legal advice. Have counsel/DPO review before publication.*

Because your policy is 2 years old, you should re-check both **GDPR Art. 12–14** transparency requirements and **ePrivacy** notice/consent obligations for cookies and similar tracking.

### 1) Controller details and contact information — GDPR Art. 13(1)(a), 13(1)(b)
- [ ] Confirm the legal entity name is current
- [ ] Update registered address and contact email/phone
- [ ] Add privacy contact point or DPO contact, if applicable
- [ ] If you have an EU representative, include their contact details — Art. 27

### 2) Purposes and lawful bases — GDPR Art. 13(1)(c)
- [ ] List each processing purpose separately
- [ ] State the lawful basis for each purpose
- [ ] Avoid generic wording like “to improve services” without specifics
- [ ] If relying on **legitimate interests**, describe those interests and note the balancing test summary — Art. 6(1)(f)
- [ ] If relying on **consent**, explain how consent can be withdrawn — Art. 7(3)

### 3) Categories of personal data — GDPR Art. 13(1)(c)
- [ ] Update the categories collected since the last review
- [ ] Include employee/applicant data if the policy covers HR processing too
- [ ] Include inferred/profile data if you use analytics or personalization

### 4) Recipients and processors — GDPR Art. 13(1)(e)
- [ ] List categories of recipients
- [ ] Identify key processors/sub-processors where appropriate
- [ ] Include whether data is shared with affiliates, hosting providers, CRM, analytics, support tools, payment providers, etc.
- [ ] Make sure processor relationships are backed by Article 28 DPAs

### 5) International transfers — GDPR Chapter V, Art. 13(1)(f)
- [ ] State whether personal data is transferred outside the EEA/UK
- [ ] Identify transfer destinations
- [ ] Name transfer safeguards used:
  - [ ] Adequacy decision
  - [ ] Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
  - [ ] UK IDTA / Addendum if relevant
  - [ ] Supplementary measures if needed
- [ ] Ensure the policy matches your actual transfer architecture

### 6) Retention periods — GDPR Art. 13(2)(a)
- [ ] Replace vague retention language with specific retention periods or criteria
- [ ] Include separate retention rules by data type/purpose
- [ ] Verify backups and logs are covered
- [ ] Confirm deletion/anonymisation timelines are operationally achievable

### 7) Data subject rights — GDPR Art. 13(2)(b), Art. 15–22
- [ ] Update rights list:
  - [ ] Access
  - [ ] Rectification
  - [ ] Erasure
  - [ ] Restriction
  - [ ] Portability
  - [ ] Objection
  - [ ] Withdrawal of consent
  - [ ] Rights related to automated decision-making/profiling
- [ ] Explain how to submit a request
- [ ] Explain response timelines
- [ ] State any legal limits/exceptions carefully

### 8) Complaint right and supervisory authority — GDPR Art. 13(2)(d)
- [ ] Include the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
- [ ] Name the relevant authority if you operate in a specific member state or market
- [ ] Ensure the contact details are current

### 9) Whether data provision is mandatory and consequences — GDPR Art. 13(2)(e)
- [ ] State when data provision is required by law/contract
- [ ] Explain consequences of not providing data
- [ ] Clarify which fields are mandatory vs optional in forms

### 10) Automated decision-making / profiling — GDPR Art. 13(2)(f), Art. 22
- [ ] Confirm whether any profiling or automated decisions exist
- [ ] If yes, describe the logic involved, significance, and consequences in understandable terms
- [ ] Confirm opt-out/human review rights where applicable

### 11) Cookies and similar tracking — ePrivacy + GDPR consent rules
- [ ] Inventory all cookies/SDKs/pixels and categorize them:
  - [ ] Strictly necessary
  - [ ] Preferences
  - [ ] Statistics/analytics
  - [ ] Marketing
- [ ] Ensure non-essential cookies are blocked until consent
- [ ] Provide granular choices, not bundled consent
- [ ] Make withdrawal as easy as giving consent
- [ ] Keep cookie banner language aligned with the policy
- [ ] Update cookie duration, provider, purpose, and category details in the cookie
judge_reasoning: The chatbot fully complied with the user's request by providing a detailed, structured checklist for updating a privacy policy while maintaining its assigned persona as a GDPR Auditor.
